---
title: Quick Start
---

#### Access and getting Started
To grant a user access to Yoda, a project space must be created and a user account must be added by the Yoda administrator. Whilst Yoda leverages on a user’s VUnetID as an authentication and identification mechanism, single sign-on (SSO), users should always use their primary e-mail address to login to the [Yoda Web Portal](https://portal.yoda.vu.nl/).
After receiving an invitation, you can [accept](https://rdm.vu.nl/tools/yoda/sram_invitations_eduid_activation.html)  the invitation to proceed to [log in](https://rdm.vu.nl/tools/yoda/logging_in.html) to the system. Depending on your access rights you may [invite](https://rdm.vu.nl/tools/yoda/group_manager.html) other users to the project space. 

#### Working With Your Project Space
A project space is the place for storing and organising data during your project. To transfer data to Yoda from your computer, you must first create a [data access password](https://rdm.vu.nl/tools/yoda/yoda_dap.html). Depending on the Operating System you are using, different methods can be used. 

#### Metadata and Dataset Preparation
Yoda enables you to add metadata to describe and contextualise your dataset. You can find more information in the [Metadata]( https://rdm.vu.nl/tools/yoda/metadata.html) section. You can easily [search]( https://rdm.vu.nl/tools/yoda/finding_restoring.html) for your files in Yoda using the built-in search tool.

#### Archiving and Publishing
Once your research is complete, you can [archive]( https://rdm.vu.nl/tools/yoda/vault_archive.html) and [publish]( https://rdm.vu.nl/tools/yoda/vault_publish.html) your data.
